air Political Practices Commissiort

Executivl! I legal 
322-5901 
January 8, 1985 
Enforcement 
322-6441 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-84-320 
Dear Mr. Briggs: 
You have requested our advice with regard to your reporting 
requirement? under the campaign provisions of the Political 
Reform Act.i./ 
As we understand them the facts are that you 
retained legal counsel to represent you with regard to a special 
city council meeting dealing with your private practice as an 
architect and contractor. 
At the time of the special council 
meeting, you were a member of the city council. 
ANALYSIS 
The Commission has held that legal expenses which are 
incurred in matters which relate to the officeholder's status or 
duties as an officeholder should be reported as campaign 
expenditures. 
Funds raised for such purposes are to be treated 
as campaign contributions and are to be reported accordingly. 
I 
enclose copies of advice letters which discuss the reasoning 
more fully. 
Should you desire specific assistance in filling 
out the campaign forms, please do not hesitate to contact 
Ms. Helen Arriola in our Los Angeles Office at (213) 620-5196. 
REL:plh 
cc. 
Helen Arriola 
-Sincerely,

Daniel L. Briggs 
12600 Central Avenue 
Chino, CA 
91710 
December 11, 1984 
Ms. Barbara A. Milman, General Counsel 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
State of California 
1100 "K" Street Building 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Reference: 
Request for Opinion and Advise/Government Code Section 
83114. 
Dear Ms. Milman, 
Enclosed please find a letter outlining specific circumstances of 
which I wish your opinion and advise. 
Your careful consideration at your earliest convenience would be 
greatly appreciated. 
Respectfully submitted, 
Daniel L. Briggs 


Daniel L. Briggs 
12600 Central Avenue 
Chino, CA 
91710 
December 11, 1984 
Mr. Jimmy Guiterrez, City Attorney 
City of Chi no 
12616 Central Avenue 
Chino, CA 
91710 
Personal and Confidential. 
Dear Jimmy, 
During my service as a Councilman in the City of Chino certain indi-
viduals both publicly and privately made asertians as to my private 
practice as an Architect and General Contractor and created a politi-
cal controversy which culuminated in a special council meeting to 
clear the matter. 
As a result of this special council meeting I sought legal counsel 
and incurred substantial costs. 
Please advise me as to my responsibility for reporting these expenses. 
In the eventuality that my campaign committee is required to report 
such expenses, do they qualify as appropriate expenditures as defined 
in section 82025 Chapter 2? 
Respectfully submitted, 
Daniel L. Briggs 


tate of Califorllia

Administration 
322·5660 
Executive/legal 
322·5901 
December 28, 1984 
Re: 
A-84-320 
Enforcement 
322-6441 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political 
Reform Act has been referred to Robert E. Leidigh, an 
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at 
(916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex 
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to 
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21 
working days. 
BAM:plh 
Very truly yours, 
!

General Counsel 


QUESTIONAIRE 
1. What in your opinion are the greatest assets of our community? 
2. What, in your opinion, are our greatest liabilities? 
3. Which of the following statements best indicate your feeling about growth in Stockton. 
A. 
Our city should not expand beyond it's present boundries. 
B. 
Our city should review it's present boundries and allow residential and commercial 
development on an as needed basis. 
C. 
Our city should be allowed to grow but only after services such as schools and city 
services are in place. 
Comments: 
4. What are some steps that you feel the city council should take to encourage business and industrial 
expansion in Stockton? 
5. Are you satisfied with Stockton's means of electing it's councilpersons by district? Yes 
NoD 
6. Please indicate by number how you would prioritize city spending. 
___ Parks 
___ Streets 
___ Police 
Comments: 
___ Refuse Pick Up 
___ Paramedic Services 
___ Traffic Controls 
___ Weed Abatement 
___ library Services 
___ Other 


ENERAL FUND 
1984 - 85 Budget by Function

FIRE

MANAGER, FiNANCE, ATTORNEY 8 PERSONNEL) 6.61 % l

It is a most difficult and frustrating task to implement a multi-million dollar 
city budget. As you are aware it is a time for setting priorities, for understan-
ding restraint and limitations. One of the main reasons for this newsletter is 
to solicit your advice through the enclosed questionaire so that I can best 
represent your interests in City Hall. As you know democracy is a complex 
chain and the most vital link is that of communication between the elected 
representatives and his or her constituency. In the past three years I have 
held over a dozen town-hall meetings in the district in order to solicit advice 
and counsel. I value your comments at any time. 
tX~c) «~~ 
Home - 473-3112 
City Hall - 944-8244 


There are three recreational parks in my 
district ... Cortez, Valverde, and Dentoni Park. 
How unfortunate it is that most famiiies living 
near their neighborhood parks have been har-
rassed by offensive young punks who, in many 
cases, do not live in the neighborhood. This city, 
in many cases, has been forced to close their 
resfrooms in the parks, due to vandalism. I have 
consistantly opposed building new parks until we 
are able to patrol and upgrade our existing ones. 
Mayor Ronk reviewing constituent correspondence 
with secretory Kathy Glick. 
Our youths are, without a doubt, our most 
valuable assets. As a father, I join in your frustrations 
that there are no recreational facilities in our district. I 
hope that in the near 
re, our 
will respond to 
this need by buildi 
a recreation center, possibly in 
one of our existing 
This wou 
allow safe and 
productive activities 
Idren. 
Mayor Ronk with son Bryon 
After several attempts in recent years, I am 
pleased to report that the city council has finally ap-
proved paramedic services in North East Stockton. 
Engine Company No. 11, which which is located near 
Swain Road and Tam 0' Shanter, is now equipped and 
as a paramedic Engine Company.

sections.question_synthetic: Must a city council member report legal expenses incurred to defend against political controversy regarding his private practice as an architect and contractor, and if so, do these qualify as appropriate campaign expenditures under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Legal expenses related to matters concerning the officeholder's status or duties must be reported as campaign expenditures, and funds raised for such purposes must be treated as campaign contributions and reported accordingly.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, ocr; LLM: Document contains multiple embedded pieces: the incoming request letter (December 11, 1984), FPPC acknowledgment letter (December 28, 1984), and formal FPPC advice response (January 8, 1985). Last pages appear to be unrelated Stockton city materials (budget questionnaire and mayor's newsletter) and were excluded from analysis. Main Q/C sections extracted from appropriate FPPC response letter dated January 8, 1985.

embedding.summary: Daniel L. Briggs, a Chino city council member, sought FPPC advice regarding whether legal expenses incurred to defend against political controversy over his private architectural and contracting practice must be reported as campaign expenditures. The FPPC concluded that such legal expenses directly related to an officeholder's status or duties must be reported as campaign expenditures.
